questions.question: Chaos engineering principles — что critical для responsible chaos testing?

options.text: Break stuff и see what happens

(1) Define hypothesis перед testing (expected vs actual); (2) Limit blast radius — start small (one TM, не cluster); (3) Have rollback plan; (4) Document expected vs actual для learnings; (5) Iterate — fix issues found, retest; (6) Staging first, production только в game days с safety; (7) Reconciliation verifies data loss или integrity issues

questions.explanation: Chaos engineering — structured testing, не destruction. Principles ensure value extraction without harm. Hypothesis: 'killing TM should recover в RTO 2 min с zero data loss' — testable, measurable. Blast radius limits — one TM not whole cluster. Rollback plan ensures recovery if test goes worse than expected. Documentation captures learnings для future. Iteration — fix issues, retest, verify. Staging first ensures safety. Reconciliation script (continuously running) verifies no data loss invisibly. Без principles: 'chaos' becomes 'destruction' with no learning value.

questions.question: Scenario: Kill JobManager (leader) в staging chaos test. Что expected behavior, и какие verification критичны?

options.text: Expected: K8s lease released (~15s), standby JM acquires leadership, reads JobGraph + checkpoint pointer из S3 HA storage, restarts job (coordinates с TMs to restore from checkpoint). Recovery 60-180s. Verification: (1) Reconciliation source = sink (no data loss); (2) No duplicates в sinks (EOS works); (3) Side outputs consistent (no late events 'hole'); (4) Web UI shows restart timeline; (5) Recovery time within SLO

questions.explanation: HA design at work. Two JM pods running, only one active leader через K8s lease. На leader crash: (1) K8s lease lifetime ~15s, не renewed = released; (2) Standby acquires lease (атомарная operation); (3) Reads HA storage (S3 path) — JobGraph для job structure + checkpoint pointer; (4) Restarts job — coordinates с TMs, restores state from checkpoint; (5) Job resumes processing. Total recovery 60-180s for healthy cluster. Verification critical: data integrity (reconciliation), no duplicates (EOS), no side output gap, timeline visible.

questions.question: Chaos test result: source 1,000,000 events, sink Paimon 1,000,032. EOS supposedly enabled. 32 extra events discrepancy. Diagnosis approach?

options.text: Multiple hypotheses: (1) Sink not truly idempotent (verify merge-engine=deduplicate, primary-key, EXACTLY_ONCE delivery guarantee explicitly); (2) Checkpoint partial commit (JM logs for inconsistencies); (3) 2PC transaction recommit bug; (4) Sink config defaulting at-least-once. Diagnostics: query duplicates SQL, Paimon snapshot history, sink subtask metrics. Fix the bug, retest

questions.explanation: 32 extra events ≠ EOS. Real diagnosis required. Hypotheses by probability: (1) Sink config — most common gotcha. Paimon needs merge-engine=deduplicate + primary-key + explicit EXACTLY_ONCE delivery guarantee. Default might be at-least-once. Verify через test query SELECT order_id, COUNT(*) GROUP BY having > 1. (2) Checkpoint partial commit — some operators succeeded, others timed out. JM logs 'checkpoint partial failure' messages. (3) 2PC transaction recommit — if commit notification lost (JM crashed между barrier and notification), recovery commits twice if state imperfect. Paimon snapshot history shows 'recovered transaction'. (4) Version compatibility bugs. Critical lesson: EOS bugs surface only under failure scenarios. Chaos testing catches them — this is the exact value proposition.

questions.question: 'Мы chaos-тестировали один раз' — достаточно для production-ready streaming pipeline.
questions.explanation: Chaos testing — ongoing requirement, не one-time. Production environments evolve: new features added, scale changes, infrastructure migrations, dependencies upgraded. Annual chaos coverage should include all critical scenarios. New incident types discovered в production add to chaos suite. 'We chaos-tested it once' — false sense of security. Production maturity practices: weekly staging chaos для key scenarios, monthly broader coverage, quarterly production game days. Mature companies (Netflix Chaos Monkey origin) treat chaos as routine, not exceptional. Without ongoing testing — drift between tested и actual production behavior expands invisibly.
